name: Critical path and PERT analysis

problemSignals.description: Delivery dates move without a traceable dependency explanation.
Multiple activities wait on shared predecessors or scarce resources.

requiredEvidence.description: Activities with explicit predecessor relationships and completion criteria.
Optimistic, most-likely, and pessimistic durations or justified fixed durations.

assumptions: Duration estimates use a consistent unit and scope

deterministicSteps.action: Validate activity identities, predecessor closure, acyclicity, and duration units.
Compute expected durations, earliest/latest times, slack, and critical paths using declared formulas.

outputs.description: Closed dependency graph with normalized duration evidence.
Expected schedule, slack, critical activities, and uncertainty assumptions.

whenAll: Duration ranges are calibrated from comparable work
Some durations rely on expert estimates
The graph is incomplete or cyclic

contraindications: Work cannot be decomposed into dependency-linked activities
Resource contention dominates but is absent from the model

contract: Emit formulas, slack, and all critical paths

source.title: Critical Path Method and Program Evaluation and Review Technique
